goal: Collapse gad (CLI+TUI as one surface) install paths to ONE canonical — no fallback. Operator 2026-04-22: 'we should never get confused about our install paths and how our users are and what paths should be needed.' Today three compete: (a) install-gad-windows.ps1 -Artifact <path-to-gad-vX.exe>, (b) 'gad install self [--dir <path>]' from packaged binary, (c) 'gad self build && gad self install' local-dogfood. Different cwd/deps/failure shapes + no Mac/Linux. Action: (1) pick ONE canonical path — propose install-gad-windows.ps1 for Windows + install-gad-unix.sh for macOS/Linux (both downloaded from GitHub Release alongside the binary), (2) retire the other two paths — 'gad install self' becomes internal-only (used by the PS1 script after download), 'gad self install' deleted or relabeled as dev-only with clear doc string, (3) install script ALWAYS installs both gad.exe and gad-tui.exe together (per 63-50 — they are one product), errors if either is missing from the downloaded artifact, (4) single canonical doc in CLAUDE.md: 'Install GAD: download from GitHub Release, run install-gad-<platform>.<ext>.' One path, one command, one install location. Deletes 2 code paths.
